title: pi-context update — a unified, drift-aware, customization-preserving update command with --dry-run
description: A single `pi-context update` command brings an installed substrate's model (schemas + catalog-origin blocks) to a newer pi-context release while preserving the user's customizations: it consults the checkStatus drift classification, preserves or merges locally-modified schemas instead of overwriting (FGAP-046), surfaces every mutation it makes, and with --dry-run previews the full change set without writing. It supersedes the bare `/context install --update` flag as the user-facing update path, and states the guarantee that config registries, populated blocks, and user-added elements are never clobbered.
motivation: The framework exists to be customized; an update must not punish customization. Today update is a flag on install that overwrites locally-modified schemas, side-writes migration declarations unsurfaced, and never consults the drift detector that already exists. A unified command with a dry-run preview makes update safe and legible.
